news.headline: NeurAxis Declares Q2 2026 Stock Dividend on Series B Preferred Stock
news.summary: The board declared a Q2 2026 stock dividend on the Series B Preferred, payable in roughly 80,463 common shares on July 29 to holders of record July 21. This is routine non-cash preferred housekeeping that adds a small ongoing dilution drip rather than any operational news.

news.headline: NeurAxis, Inc. Added to Russell 3000E Growth Benchmark
news.summary: NRXS was added to the Russell 3000E Growth benchmark in FTSE Russell's annual reconstitution, broadening passive-fund ownership and index visibility. The inclusion followed a 52-week high of $9.33 hit on June 27 amid the post-CPT-code rally.

news.headline: NeurAxis Stockholders Approve Directors and Equity Compensation Plans
news.summary: At the June 10 annual meeting shareholders elected six directors and approved an increase to the equity incentive plan plus a new employee stock purchase plan. It is a governance milestone that matters mainly as a source of additional potential stock-comp dilution.

news.headline: NRXS: Record Q1 Revenue and Expanded Coverage Drive Growth in Pediatric and VA Neuromodulation Markets
news.summary: Zacks reiterated a constructive view citing record Q1 revenue of $1.61M (+80% YoY) at roughly 86% gross margin, with expanding insurance coverage and the Veterans Affairs channel flagged as growth areas alongside pediatrics. The note frames the January Category I CPT code as the structural driver behind accelerating reimbursed volume.

news.headline: Craig-Hallum Sticks to Its Buy Rating for NeurAxis, Inc. (NRXS)
news.summary: Craig-Hallum reiterated its Buy rating on NRXS following the record Q1 print, endorsing the reimbursement-driven growth thesis after the new Category I CPT code took effect. The sell-side support reinforced the bullish momentum that carried the stock to late-June highs.

news.headline: NeurAxis Expands Payer Coverage with Four New Medical Policies
news.summary: NeurAxis announced four additional health insurers adopted medical policies covering PENFS/IB-Stim, adding roughly 1.25 million covered lives across West Virginia, New Hampshire, South Carolina, Virginia and Florida. The expansion continues the steady build-out of reimbursed access that underpins the company's revenue growth thesis.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings
catalysts.detail: NeurAxis's next quarterly report is broadly expected around August 11, 2026 (per earnings-calendar trackers; not yet formally confirmed by the company) and will show whether the Category I CPT-code tailwind is sustaining 70-80%+ revenue growth and continued burn reduction.

catalysts.label: Commercial payer coverage expansion
catalysts.detail: Additional positive medical policies from major commercial insurers would directly increase covered lives (already ~100M nationally) and reimbursed IB-Stim volume; management's highest strategic priority.

catalysts.label: Path to ~$15M revenue / cash-flow breakeven
catalysts.detail: Management targets roughly $15M annual revenue for cash-flow breakeven with burn guided to $1M or less for the rest of 2026, reducing near-term dilution risk.

catalysts.label: Series B stock dividend payment
catalysts.detail: Declared Q2 preferred stock dividend of ~80,463 common shares pays July 29 to holders of record July 21.

pulse: As of mid-July 2026, NeurAxis remains focused on converting the January Category I CPT code into durable reimbursed growth, having posted 80% YoY Q1 revenue growth and roughly 86% gross margins while steadily widening payer coverage (four more insurer policies added in April, national coverage near 100M lives). Sentiment stayed constructive through early summer with Russell 3000E Growth index inclusion, a reiterated Craig-Hallum Buy, and a 52-week high of $9.33 in late June, though news flow in the past two weeks has been routine (annual-meeting governance items, a Series B preferred stock dividend paying July 29) with no fresh operational catalysts since. The next real test is the Q2 print expected around August 11, with the going-concern note, ATM dilution and micro-cap volatility remaining the key risks to watch.
